Type of parameter: Population mean (average total funding raised, in millions USD, across all venture-backed companies).

Known facts and data points:
• Median total funding per company often reported around 8–9 M USD (Crunchbase, CB Insights).

• Distribution is heavily right-skewed: few outliers raise hundreds of millions but are a small fraction.

Data sources and analogous populations:
• Venture-capital databases (Crunchbase, PitchBook, CB Insights) covering thousands of companies.
• Academic studies on startup financing showing skewed funding distributions.
• Industry reports indicating median vs. mean gaps due to outliers.

Key factors influencing the mean:
• Stage distribution: many companies only raise seed or A.
• Geographic mix: US startups often raise more than those in other regions.
• Time period: funding rounds have grown over the last decade.
• Survivorship bias: highly funded companies more visible in datasets.

Limitations, uncertainties, and assumptions:
• Reporting bias: not all rounds disclosed.
• Changing market conditions: recent funding booms/busts.
• Definition of “venture-backed” may vary.
• I assume a broad, current sample across regions and stages.

Subgroup differences and temporal change:
• US vs. Europe vs. Asia average funding differs by 10–50%.
• Recent years (2020–2021) saw larger rounds than earlier periods.

Best guess and uncertainty estimate:
• Best guess (mean) ≈ 10 M USD.
• Standard deviation (uncertainty about this mean) ≈ 3 M USD.

Justification of choices:
• Anchored on median ~8–9 M, skew pushing mean higher.
• Balanced low-stage majority vs. high-stage outliers.
• Uncertainty ±3 M captures plausible variation in data sources, time periods, definitions.

Interpretation of uncertainty:
• 95% credible interval ≈ 10 ± 6 M (4 M to 16 M) reflects moderate uncertainty.
• Uncertainty pertains to our estimate of the true average funding, not to individual company funding variability.

Plausible edge cases:
• If dataset over-samples unicorns, mean could approach 15 M.
• If many micro-VC rounds included, mean could be as low as 5 M.

</justification>

<confidence_level>
Moderately uncertain. While median figures and stage averages are reasonably well-known, data coverage, skewness, and evolving funding trends introduce notable uncertainty about the true average.
